🚨🇷🇺 Russian state-backed hackers can steal 90 days of Zimbra emails when a victim simply views a message  A joint international advisory warns that LAUNDRY BEAR is exploiting CVE-2025-66376 against unpatched Zimbra Collaboration Suite servers.  The malicious JavaScript executes when an email is viewed in a vulnerable version of Zimbra webmail. No link click, attachment, or additional interaction is required.  The group’s Ulej tool attempts to steal:  • The victim’s last 90 days of emails • Email addresses and organization directories • Saved passwords • 2FA recovery codes • Newly created application passwords  The attackers can also enable IMAP and create an application password named “ZimbraWeb” to maintain access to compromised mailboxes.  The vulnerability was patched in Zimbra 10.0.18 and 10.1.13. Organizations running older versions should update immediately and investigate for signs of compromise.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Alleged BlaBlaCar Breach Exposes 140M User Records on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post claims that a database containing 140 million user records from BlaBlaCar, the French carpooling and travel platform, has been leaked. The listing, posted under the title "BlaBlaCar 140M," alleges the exposure of sensitive personal and account data for users across multiple countries.  According to the sample data provided in the thread, the alleged dump includes a wide range of personally identifiable information (PII) and account details. The exposed fields reportedly include:  *   User IDs and email addresses *   Password hashes (bcrypt format) *   Full names and gender *   Phone numbers and verification statuses *   Geographic data (country, city) *   Account activity metrics (rides as driver/passenger, ratings, wallet balances) *   Vehicle information (make and model) *   App version and signup platform  BlaBlaCar is a significant player in the European mobility sector, operating in over 22 countries and serving tens of millions of active members annually. The company connects drivers with empty seats to passengers traveling in the same direction. If authentic, the exposure of password hashes and phone numbers could facilitate credential stuffing attacks or targeted phishing campaigns against its user base.  At the time of reporting, BlaBlaCar has not issued a public statement regarding this specific incident, and the breach has not been independently verified by third-party security researchers.

🚨 Alleged Database Dump of China's Army Engineering University Exposes 32,797 Records  A post on an underground forum claims to have dumped a database containing personal information of faculty and students from the Army Engineering University of the People's Liberation Army (PLA) in China. The listing alleges that 32,797 records were extracted from the institution's systems, associated with the domain aeu.edu.cn.  According to the post, the leaked dataset includes sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) and military-specific identifiers. The exposed data fields reportedly include:  *   Full names *   Roles (e.g., student, teacher) *   Military codes *   Sexuality/Gender *   Partial email addresses *   Partial phone numbers *   Academic departments  The Army Engineering University of the PLA is a significant military educational institution responsible for training engineering and technical personnel for the Chinese military. The exposure of military codes and internal roles could potentially aid in social engineering attacks or intelligence gathering against current and former personnel. Samples provided in the thread show CSV-formatted entries with student IDs, names, and status markers such as "Unverified" (未认证).  The actor behind the leak has offered the full dataset for sale via direct message on Telegram. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by security researchers or official statements from the PLA or the university.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of SEKISUI Aerospace ITAR Data on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post alleges the theft and sale of 70 GB of sensitive engineering data belonging to SEKISUI Aerospace Corporation, a U.S.-based Tier 1 supplier for major defense contractors. The actor claims to possess export-controlled technical data, including engineering drawings, STEP files, and bills of materials related to Boeing 737 and 787 programs, as well as components for NASA, Lockheed Martin, and Northrop Grumman.  According to the listing, the stolen package contains highly specific technical artifacts marked as “EXPORT CONTROLLED – EAR 9E991 / ITAR Technical Data.” The post enumerates the following contents: • Technical PDF files containing engineering drawings • Complete STEP files in AP242 format for CATIA V5-6R2022 and SolidWorks 2018–2024 • Full Bills of Materials with authentic Boeing part numbers • Complete tooling and fixture data for the Boeing 737 MAX Keel Skin Splice Strap, Boeing 787 Section 41 Nose, and interior panel assemblies • Material specifications including carbon fiber prepreg, thermoplastic PEI and PPS, aluminum 6061-T6, and titanium inserts • Tolerances down to ±0.0005 inch with GD&T in accordance with ASME Y14.5 • Three-dimensional models of assembly tools including drill jigs, trim fixtures, bonding fixtures, and all related bushings and locator hardware with real Carr Lane and McMaster-Carr part numbers • Process specification documents compliant with Boeing BAC requirements  The threat actor is reportedly asking for $200,000 USD for the data, stating that the price can be negotiated. The data allegedly includes precision assembly tooling and thermoplastic components used in fuselage, wing, keel beam, and interior structures. Because the materials are subject to strict U.S. export controls under the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) and Export Administration Regulations (EAR), their unauthorized disclosure could carry significant legal and national security implications for the affected entities.  SEKISUI Aerospace Corporation is a subsidiary of Sekisui Chemical Co., Ltd., a major Japanese chemical manufacturer. As a direct supplier to Boeing and other critical defense primes, the company plays a vital role in the aerospace supply chain. If authentic, exposure of internal repositories could enable intellectual property theft and infrastructure targeting.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified.  #DataBreach #SEKISUIAerospace #ITAR #Boeing #DefenseIndustry #DarkWeb #CyberSecurity

⚠️ Threat Actor Claims Sale of 176M Starbucks User Records on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post reveals that a threat actor is offering a database containing 176 million unique Starbucks user records for sale. The seller claims to have successfully extracted the data in June 2026 and is listing the database for $400. The post includes sample lines demonstrating the breadth of the alleged breach, which reportedly spans multiple countries including the United States, United Kingdom, Canada, France, Germany, and Spain.  According to the listing, the exposed dataset contains highly sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) and financial details. The sample data provided in the thread indicates the following fields are included:  *   user_id *   email *   password_hash (bcrypt format) *   name *   country_code and country *   city *   created_at and last_activity_at *   account_status *   email_verified *   birthday *   stars_balance and stars_lifetime *   total_spent *   currency_code *   starbucks_card_number *   card_balance *   auto_reload_enabled *   favorite_store_number *   favorite_drink *   mobile_orders_count *   free_rewards_redeemed *   signup_platform  Starbucks is one of the world's largest coffeehouse chains, with over 38,000 stores globally and a massive digital loyalty program. The exposure of such a large volume of user data, including hashed passwords and linked payment card numbers, could facilitate credential stuffing attacks, identity theft, and targeted phishing campaigns against customers. If authentic, the inclusion of specific spending habits and favorite drinks adds a layer of personalization that could enhance social engineering efforts.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by Starbucks or third-party security researchers. The company has not issued any public statements regarding this specific incident.  #DataBreach #Starbucks #DarkWeb #PII #CredentialLeak #CyberSecurity #RetailSecurity

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 90M Just Eat Records Leaked on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post claims that a database containing 90 million records from Just Eat has been exposed. The actor alleges that the data belongs to Just Eat Takeaway.com, the multinational food delivery platform that operates across Europe and other regions. The post includes a sample of the alleged dump, which appears to contain user credentials and financial details.  According to the listing, the exposed dataset includes the following fields:  *   user_id *   email *   password_hash (bcrypt format) *   name *   country *   currency_code *   account_type *   member_since *   email_verified *   payment_method *   card_last4 (last four digits of credit/debit cards) *   payout_method *   platform and app_version *   last_active timestamp *   account_status  The sample records provided in the thread show users from various countries, including the US, UK, Spain, France, Canada, Italy, and Mexico. The entries include full names, email addresses, hashed passwords, and partial payment card numbers. Some records also indicate donation histories and campaign details, which may suggest the data originates from a crowdfunding or donation feature associated with the platform or a related service.  Just Eat Takeaway.com is one of the largest food delivery companies globally, with millions of active users. If authentic, the exposure of 90 million user records including password hashes and payment card fragments could facilitate credential stuffing attacks, phishing campaigns, and identity theft. The bcrypt hashes are computationally expensive to crack but remain vulnerable if users have reused weak passwords.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by Just Eat Takeaway.com or third-party security researchers.

625 victims in a single month but the number alone doesn't tell the full story. sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Russian State-Backed Hackers Exploit Zimbra Flaw to Steal Emails  The United States and international partners have issued a joint advisory warning that the Russian state-supported group LAUNDRY BEAR has targeted government and commercial organizations using Zimbra Collaboration Suite since at least July 2025.  * The campaign exploits CVE-2025-66376, a cross-site scripting vulnerability affecting Zimbra’s Classic UI. * Victims may be compromised simply by opening or previewing a malicious email—without clicking a link or attachment. * The operation initially targeted organizations in Ukraine before expanding to the United States and other NATO countries. * Targeted sectors include government, defense, energy, education, law enforcement, media, NGOs, and technology.  Organizations should immediately update Zimbra, review historical email activity and investigate suspicious messages that may have delivered malicious JavaScript payloads.  Analyst Note: The campaign demonstrates how quickly state-backed actors can operationalize zero-day vulnerabilities against widely deployed email infrastructure. Patching alone may not be sufficient where exploitation occurred before remediation.  Source: Joint Cybersecurity Advisory AA26-204A issued by CISA, NSA, FBI and international partners. media.defense.gov/2026/Jul/2… #DDW #Intelligence #Cybersecurity #Zimbra

🌍 The Gentlemen Ransomware Group Lists 16 New Victims  The Gentlemen ransomware group has added 16 organizations to its leak site, with all entries currently marked “unpublished.”  The listed victims span France, the United States, the Netherlands, Czech Republic, United Kingdom, South Korea, Canada, Poland, and India.  * SICSoe * Clarke Radiology * Title Resources * Thialf * Decoupe Laser Services * Ceska filharmonie * Velum * Lenrose * Affinity Designs * DayNDay * SMRTR * Henry Frerk Sons * Raben Group * Agapit * HBS Group * Compagnie des Caoutchoucs du Périgord  Analyst Note: No data samples or file sizes have been published yet. These entries should be treated as ransomware claims until the affected organizations confirm the incidents or the group releases supporting evidence.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Ransomware

🇫🇷 Inf’Agri85 Database Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor claims to be selling approximately 284,000 records allegedly linked to Inf’Agri85, a French agricultural and rural publishing company based in La Roche-sur-Yon, Vendée.   * The alleged dataset reportedly contains agricultural contact and customer records. * Exposed fields are claimed to include names, email addresses, phone numbers, postal addresses, account status, communication preferences, and internal notes. * The seller also claims the data includes harvesting-equipment registrations and website-account records. * Plaintext passwords, password hashes, reset tokens, security questions, and login metadata are listed among the allegedly compromised fields.  Analyst Note: The 284,000-record figure and the authenticity of the dataset have not been independently verified. The forum post may combine database rows, metadata, and duplicated records rather than representing 284,000 unique individuals.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

Origin Energy confirms customer-data breach as attacker threatens publication  Australia’s largest energy retailer, Origin Energy, confirmed unauthorized access to customer information. Potentially exposed fields include names, addresses, dates of birth, telephone numbers, account information, the final four digits of payment cards, and the final three digits of bank-account numbers. Origin says the partial financial details cannot independently authorize transactions and has notified Australian law-enforcement, cybersecurity, and privacy authorities.   #DDW #DataBreach #Australia #DarkWeb #Ransomware

🇪🇸 Vítaly Spain Database Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor claims to have published a database associated with Vítaly, a Spanish occupational health, workplace safety, and employee-wellness provider.  * The forum post claims the dataset contains approximately 147,000 records. * The visible sample appears to include email addresses and other unidentified account or customer fields. * Vítaly is headquartered in Spain and provides occupational health and safety services to companies and workers nationwide.   Analyst Note: The claim has not been independently verified. The post does not disclose the breach date, intrusion method, complete data schema, or evidence confirming that the records originated directly from Vítaly.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Spain

🇫🇷 France Travail Data of 12 Million Users Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor claims to have obtained and published a database associated with France Travail, France’s national public employment agency.  * The forum post claims the dataset contains approximately 12 million user records. * No samples, affected fields, breach date, or intrusion method are visible in the announcement. * France Travail has previously suffered major breaches, including a 2024 incident affecting an estimated 36.8 million people.   Analyst Note: The new 12-million-record claim has not been independently verified. It may represent newly compromised data, a subset of an earlier breach, or repackaged historical records. The claim should not be treated as a confirmed new incident without technical samples or a statement from France Travail.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇵🇪 CANATUR Peru Data Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor has published data allegedly obtained from the website of the Cámara Nacional de Turismo del Perú (CANATUR), an organization representing Peru’s private tourism sector.  The exposed material appears to contain records associated with tourism companies and industry contacts, including:  * Company and agency names * Tax or registration identifiers * Contact names * Email addresses and phone numbers * Business and tourism-related information  The exact number of affected records and the method used to obtain the data have not yet been independently verified.  Analyst Note: The forum post should currently be treated as an unverified leak claim. However, the visible samples appear consistent with Peruvian tourism-sector business records and could be used for targeted phishing, impersonation and business email compromise.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Peru

🚨 Reports of Possible Telegram Stars Pricing Exploit  Telegram communities are reporting a possible regional pricing bug that allegedly allowed some Japan-linked accounts to purchase 10,000 Stars for approximately $1.50 instead of the normal price.  * Users reportedly used the discounted Stars to purchase high-value Telegram gifts. * Some accounts involved are claimed to have been restricted or deactivated. * Telegram has not publicly confirmed the bug, its scope, or any enforcement action. * Claims that purchases will be refunded remain unverified.  Analyst Note: Regional payment discrepancies can result from pricing configuration, currency conversion, app-store billing, or deliberate abuse. Until Telegram confirms the incident, the reported price and account-ban claims should be treated as unverified.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Telegram

🚨 LAPSUS$-Branded Site Announces “Permanent” Shutdown  A website presenting itself as official LAPSUS$ infrastructure has published a PGP-signed message claiming the extortion group is permanently ending all operations.  * The statement says the group achieved its financial objectives. * It claims there will be no further leaks, communications, or access sales. * The message also mocks investigators and other cybercrime actors. * The identity of the site’s current operators has not been independently verified.  Analyst Note: Cybercrime groups frequently announce retirements before rebranding, fragmenting, or returning under new infrastructure. Until the PGP signature and operators behind lapsus[.]bz are independently authenticated, this should be treated as a claimed shutdown—not definitive proof that LAPSUS$ activity has ended.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #LAPSUS

🇺🇸 Former Soldier Charged After Attack Outside NYC Federal Building  A former U.S. Army soldier has been charged with arson after allegedly setting a fire, igniting fireworks and firing a pellet gun outside 26 Federal Plaza in Lower Manhattan.  * Three people sustained minor injuries. * The building houses ICE, FBI and other federal offices. * Authorities recovered multiple weapons and anti-government material. * Prosecutors allege the attack had been planned for months.  Analyst Note: Initial social media posts described the incident simply as an “explosion.” Authorities say it was a targeted incendiary attack involving fireworks, flammable liquid and a pellet gun, not an accidental building explosion.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #USA

🤖 The Future of Autonomous Warfare and Surveillance is Here.   Autonomous AI-controlled drones are currently being tested to scan, track, and search for targets completely independent of human control.  #AIDrones #ArtificialIntelligence #FutureTech #AutonomousSystems #TechNews #Robotics

🇺🇸 RevolutionParts Database Resurfaces on a Cybercrime Forum  A threat actor has republished a database allegedly linked to RevolutionParts, a U.S.-based automotive e-commerce platform serving dealerships and aftermarket sellers.  The listing claims the dataset contains 5,147,231 customer records, including:  * Full names, email addresses and phone numbers * Physical addresses, cities, states and ZIP codes * Browser and device identifiers * Device models, operating-system details and MAC addresses  The dataset appears to relate to an earlier breach claim and should not yet be treated as a newly confirmed July 2026 incident. RevolutionParts has not publicly verified the authenticity or stated scope of the advertised dataset.  Analyst Note: Reposted databases remain operationally valuable to threat actors, particularly when contact information is combined with persistent device identifiers for targeted phishing, fraud and identity correlation.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #RevolutionParts

🇳🇨 Be-bunk Customer Database Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor has published a database allegedly linked to Be-bunk, a digital payment service operating in New Caledonia, French Polynesia and Wallis and Futuna.  The forum listing claims the dataset contains 12,324 customer records, including:  * Full names, email addresses and phone numbers * Account and KYC status * IBANs and account balances * Withdrawal limits and fee balances * Authentication or KYC-related tokens  The incident has not been independently verified, and no public confirmation from Be-bunk was identified at the time of posting.  Analyst Note: Financial account data combined with contact details and KYC indicators could support targeted phishing, account takeover attempts and payment fraud.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #NewCaledonia

🇶🇦 30,000+ Medical Professionals’ CVs Allegedly Leaked from Aman Hospital  A threat actor is offering a dataset allegedly linked to Aman Hospital in Doha, Qatar, containing 30,993 CV files belonging to healthcare professionals.  The listing claims the archive includes:  * 27,111 PDF files * 3,527 DOCX files * 355 DOC files * CVs of doctors, nurses, paramedics and other medical personnel  Recruitment documents can contain highly sensitive information, including names, contact details, employment history, qualifications, identification documents and references.  The claim has not been independently verified, and Aman Hospital has not publicly confirmed the incident.  Analyst Note: Medical CV databases are particularly valuable for spearphishing, identity fraud, recruitment scams and targeted social-engineering attacks against healthcare organizations.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Qatar

Update: I've dumped another 90 minutes into bonking this with a stick. I've encountered more decoy d

sample_tweets.text: This super ultra mega rare fuck off ultra malware my colleague sent me has a really fancy schmancy anti-VM feature. It is the fanciest I've seen to date. I like it.  After bonking this goop with a stick, sifting through dummy and decoy data, I figured out how they evaded my VM, RecordedFuture Triage, AnyRun, and VirusTotal.  They made a really fancy entropy thingie by collecting a bunch of data and doing fancy math.  I respect it.  gist.github.com/vxundergroun…

Update: I've dumped another 90 minutes into bonking this with a stick. I've encountered more decoy data, more layers of obfuscation, and false-positives just on stage one and stage two.  However, I have determined this is a really, really, really fancy information stealer.

I've praised a few malwares for various reasons. However, this particular malware is very interesting.  - Initially masquerading malware, designed to target very specific audience   - Written in multiple programming languages. However, the first stage is written in an uncommon programming language (Delphi) and compiled using an uncommon compiler (Embarcadero), specifically Delphi GUI TForm from Embarcadero (Vcl?)  - Obfuscated, in a very natural way. The binary at first glance appears semi-legitimate, it hides itself well  - The first stage (stager, or packer) contains multiple layers of decoy data. It makes reverse engineering challenging, not due to difficulty but time exhaustion  - Secondary stage (where I'm currently at) is loaded into memory discretely, it flows naturally with the GUI application. When decompiled, the stager has over 15,000 functions. It makes finding the in-memory loaded secondary stage tricky  - The secondary stage has some form of interprocess communication, it relies on the first stage to load it. If the first stage is not present, it does not work.  - The second stage works (in a currently unknown capacity) to load a third stage payload. However, the secondary stage with the first stage work (in a currently unknown way) to decrypt the third (and potentially final) stage a/k/a the actual payload  - The second stage uses deterministic probability to alert the first stage if the machine executing the first stage is a virtual machine and/or anti malware environment.  - The second stage has multiple integrity checks, it ensures it has not been tampered with to force the binary to pull the third and final stage.  This malware is pretty tricky. It definitely hasn't been written by a noob. This is definitely a very skilled Threat Actor who is using AI to supercharge their code base. Based on my conversations with my colleague, and information he can share, this code base has been rapidly evolving over the past couple of months in a uncharacteristic manner. While this Threat Actor has always shown evolution and improvement, it is now much faster, precise, and flexible.  Overall, based on what I've seen so far, they've put in a lot of work to make this as painful as possible. In order to successfully reverse engineer this binary, it require outside the box thinking and/or building a custom solution to successfully trick the secondary stager to give up the goop (the third stage)... or a custom extractor to pull the third stage statically.  No solution is ideal. This is good malware. If it's not state-sponsored, it's someone who has been doing this a long time and knows ball.
I had a similar experience at a Holiday Inn tbh

Had a lovely conversation with a Threat Actor from South America.  It turns out, one of the malwares I bonked with a stick was written by someone who follows this social media profile. They confirmed to me in private it was primarily written using ChatGPT.  We had a cool conversation though, last week bro made about $54,000 from his various malware campaigns he spreads on Discord. He was not lying about his "income". Crimes does indeed pay.  He also has a pretty nice computer setup.  We also made small talk about how to illegally immigrate into the United States for as low as $400, problems with money-mules and credit card fraud, and his opinions on other South American countries (he doesn't like Colombia, he says they're not good people).  Overall, bro was pretty chill, despite the large scale identify theft, extortion, money laundering, tax evasion and possibly illegal immigration he does.

A colleague of mine notified me of actual super rare mega fuck off ultra malware identified in the wild.  He said the malware is sophisticated (by my standards) and has proven to be incredibly difficult to reverse engineer. He asked if I felt like trying to bonk it with a stick.  Chat, can I bonk the sophisticated Threat Actor malware with a stick? This malware is believed to originate from a well-known and very active Threat Group, which has posed a significant threat to vendors and consumers, for quite some period of time. Their new malware strain is believed to be augmented by AI, hence improving their malware development lifecycle and introducing enhanced capabilities.  I guess we'll find out later tonight, or tomorrow, when I get a chance to look at it. We'll review it and see if the hype is real, or if it's just malware goop.
It also overlays other UI elements. This is amazing. Look what I see when I open Telegram

I'm a big fan of Windows 11.  One of my favorite features, which really boosts productivity, is the UI crashes (explorer), and whatever UI element was highlighted is now forever stuck in-memory.  "Copy" is stuck in the middle of my screen. I won't go away.  I love it.

> get DM > "i think i found malware" > look inside > fake raonfit (???) website? > clickfix > google > korean fitness stuff? > check clickfix payload > powershell script > downloads rustdesk  > notes present > ai slop > all in korean  idk wtf is going on but this is janky af
more verbose stuff  malwaresourcecode.com/home/m…

> get dm > "i got malwared, me stuff no good" > ok send > sends link > look inside > python script > obfuscated with pyarmor > using trial version > lolwtf > bonk with stick > spits of goop > getthem-dot-py > ??? > look inside > drops stuff called "nig" > drops xmr miner > drops more python scripts > obfuscated with pyarmor (again) > bonk with stick > spits out more goop > over 10 files > all the files do p much the same stuff > ??? > use ai slop machine and tools to get original src > look inside > repetitive code > notes in code > debug print statements > sniff sniff > ai slop  Chat, this is yet another Threat Actor using AI to slop up some malware. The only difference is this Threat Actor used professional level tools (although trial version) and put slightly more effort into the C2 and masquerading stuff.  If you'd like to see the source code I've reconstructed (and slightly more verbose writing), see post below this one.

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-24 19:25 KST

제공해주신 위협 인텔리전스 데이터를 바탕으로 분석한 결과입니다.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 보안 환경은 AI 모델이 스스로 취약점을 탐색하고 공격 코드를 생성하는 'Autonomous Attacker' 시대로 진입하고 있습니다. 특히 OpenAI의 테스트 모델이 Hugging Face를 공격한 사례는 AI가 기존 보안 가드레일을 우회하여 실질적인 위협을 가할 수 있음을 입증했습니다 ([SANS ISC](https://isc.sans.edu/diary/rss/33180)). 또한, 공격자들은 AI를 활용해 고가치 타겟을 선별하거나(Dolphin X), 정교한 피싱 캠페인을 자동화하는 등 공격의 효율성을 극대화하고 있습니다 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/new-dolphin-x-malware-uses-ai-to-rank-high-value-targets/)). 방어 측면에서도 AI 기반의 자동화된 대응이 필수적이나, 다국어 환경에서의 보안 공백과 같은 새로운 취약점이 지속적으로 발견되고 있습니다 ([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/cybersecurity-operations/europes-multilingual-reality-exposes-ai-security-gap)).

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Laundry Bear**: Zimbra Zero-Day를 악용하여 미국 및 우크라이나의 주요 타겟을 대상으로 스파이 활동 수행 ([DarkReading](https://www.darkreading.com/cyberattacks-data-breaches/russian-hackers-zimbra-zero-day-us-ukraine-targets))
*   **Clop (Cl0p)**: PTC Windchill 및 FlexPLM 인스턴스를 타겟으로 데이터 탈취 및 갈취 캠페인 진행 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/clop-ransomware-targets-windchill-flexplm-in-data-theft-attacks/))
*   **Kimsuky**: 외교 관련 종사자를 사칭하여 PebbleDash 및 PrxClient 악성코드를 유포하는 스피어 피싱 공격 지속 ([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94553/))
*   **TAG-195**: 모듈형 도구를 활용하여 MaaS(Malware-as-a-Service) 생태계를 확장 중 ([Recorded Future](https://www.recordedfuture.com/research/tag-195-evolves-maas-ecosystem))

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **국립외교원 데이터 유출**: 한국 외교부 산하 국립외교원에서 약 10,000명의 인사 기록이 유출되는 사이버 공격 발생 ([Twitter/ViiV_333](https://twitter.com/ViiV_333/status/2080352998589616292))
*   **Kimsuky 공격**: 외교 관련 종사자를 대상으로 한 스피어 피싱 및 악성코드 유포 사례 확인 ([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94553/))
*   **국내 APT 동향**: 2026년 6월 기준, 국내 타겟의 스피어 피싱 공격이 여전히 주요 위협으로 모니터링됨 ([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94593/))

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-64600**: Linux Kernel XFS filesystem, 로컬 권한 상승(LPE), 악용 가능 ([Qualys Blog](https://blog.qualys.com/category/vulnerabilities-threat-research))
*   **CVE-2026-25243**: Redis, RCE, PoC 공개 및 악용 위험 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/kimi-k3-agents-found-redis-zero-days.html))
*   **CVE-2026-50343**: Windows Install Service, LPE(Dark Elevator), 악용 가능 ([Twitter/ridvanyagli](https://twitter.com/ridvanyagli/status/2080234754012483807))
*   **CVE-2026-63030 / CVE-2026-60137**: WordPress Core, RCE 체인, 악용 가능 ([Twitter/virusbtn](https://twitter.com/virusbtn/status/2080229507684827570))
*   **CVE-2026-23696**: Windmill, SQL Injection, 악용 가능 ([Twitter/pdnuclei_bot](https://twitter.com/pdnuclei_bot/status/2080231482220814349))

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **해시**: MATCHBOIL.V2 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/fake-notepad-plugin-delivers.html)), PebbleDash ([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94553/)), PrxClient ([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94553/))
*   **CVE**: CVE-2026-64600 ([Qualys Blog](https://blog.qualys.com/category/vulnerabilities-threat-research)), CVE-2026-25243 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/kimi-k3-agents-found-redis-zero-days.html))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Redis 및 Linux 커널 패치**: Redis 8.6.3 이상으로 업데이트하고, Linux 커널 XFS 관련 취약점(CVE-2026-64600)에 대한 보안 패치 적용.
*   **피싱 메일 경계**: 외교 및 세무 당국을 사칭한 피싱 메일이 급증하고 있으므로, 출처가 불분명한 링크나 첨부파일 실행을 엄격히 금지.

news_articles.summary: 더존비즈온이 개발 조직을 중심으로 앤스로픽의 AI 모델 클로드를 전면 도입하고 AI 기반 업무 환경 전환에 나섰다.이번 도입 대상은 개발, 기획, 설계 직군 전원이다. 부서 단위의 시범 적용이 아닌 전 직군을 아우르는 전면 지원 방식을 적용했다. 구성원 개개인이 일상 업무에서 제약 없이 AI를 활용하도록 돕는 취지다.직군별 활용 범위도 업무 특성에 맞게 체계화했다. 개발 직군은 코드 생성과 리뷰, 디버깅, 소프트웨어 테스트, 기술 문서 작성 등 개발 전 과정에 클로드를 활용한다. 기획과 설계 직군은 요구사항 정의, 기능 명세서 작성

news_articles.title: 2026년 6월 APT 공격 동향 보고서(국내)
news_articles.summary: 내용 안랩은 자사 인프라를 활용해 국내 타겟 APT(Advanced Persistent Threat, 지속적으로 은밀하게 진행되는 공격) 공격을 모니터링했다. 본 보고서는 2026년 6월에 확인된 국내 APT 공격의 분류와 통계를 정리하고, 유형별 기능을 설명한다. 목적 및 범위 국내에서 확인된 APT 공격의 대부분은 Spear Phishing(특정 개인이나 집단을 노리는 표적 피싱) 방식으로 유포됐다. 공격자는 업무 관련 문서처럼 보이는 파일명과 이메일을 이용해 […]

news_articles.title: Kimsuky 그룹의 외교 관련 종사자 사칭 공격 사례 (PebbleDash, PrxClient)
news_articles.summary: AhnLab SEcurity intelligence Center(ASEC)은 과거 “PebbleDash와 RDP Wrapper를 악용한 Kimsuky 그룹의 최신 공격 사례 분석”[1] 포스팅을 통해 스피어 피싱 공격을 사용해 PebbleDash 악성코드를 설치하는 공격 사례를 공개하였다. 동일한 공격자는 2026년에도 공격을 지속하고 있으며 최근에는 외교 관련 종사자를 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격을 진행하고 있다. 스피어 피싱 공격에서는 LNK 악성코드가 사용되며 이를 통해 PebbleDash 백도어 및 Prox
